title: Divide by 6
strategies: Think of half-dozens (6 at a time).

learn.title: Sharing Into 6 Equal Groups
learn.content: When you divide by 6, you share a total equally among 6 groups. Example: 36 counters shared into 6 groups → each group gets 6. So 36 ÷ 6 = 6.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.6 'Divide by 6' pp.285-288
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 6
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Array: 6 Rows
learn.content: A 6×8 array has 48 tiles arranged in 6 rows of 8. Reading it as division: 48 tiles in 6 rows means 48 ÷ 6 = 8 tiles per row. The ×6 fact 6×8=48 gives the ÷6 fact 48÷6=8.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.6 'Divide by 6' pp.285-288
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 6
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Break ÷6 Into ÷2 Then ÷3
learn.content:
Since 6 = 2 × 3, dividing by 6 is the same as dividing by 2 and then by 3. Example: 24 ÷ 6
Step 1: 24 ÷ 2 = 12
Step 2: 12 ÷ 3 = 4
Result: 24 ÷ 6 = 4. Check: 6 × 4 = 24 ✓

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.6 'Divide by 6' pp.285-288
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 6
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Number Line: Backward Jumps of 6
learn.content: To find 48 ÷ 6, start at 48 on a number line and hop backward by 6 each time: 48 → 42 → 36 → 30 → 24 → 18 → 12 → 6 → 0. Count the hops: 8 hops. So 48 ÷ 6 = 8.

learn.title: ÷6 검증 — 답 × 6 = 원래 수?
learn.content: ÷6 답을 구한 뒤 검산: 답 × 6 = 원래 수? 예: 48 ÷ 6 = 8 → 검산: 8 × 6 = 48 ✓. 예: 54 ÷ 6 = 9 → 검산: 9 × 6 = 54 ✓. 🔍 검증 기법: 답 × 6 = (답 × 5) + 답 (예: 8×6 = 40+8 = 48). 흔한 실수 (M02): ÷6 답을 외우지 않고 큰 수에서 시간 낭비 — ÷2→÷3 분해 또는 ×6 역연산 둘 다 효과적.

feedback.correct: Yes! 6 × 10 = 60, so each row has 10 cars.
feedback.incorrect: Divide 60 by 2 then by 3: 60÷2=30, 30÷3=10. So 60 ÷ 6 = 10 cars per row.

try.feedback_correct: Yes! 6 × 10 = 60, so each row has 10 cars.

hints: How many groups of 6 make 30? Use the 6× fact: 6 × ? = 30.

feedback.correct: Correct! 6 × 5 = 30, so there are 5 half-dozen groups.
feedback.incorrect: Divide 30 by 2 then by 3: 30÷2=15, 15÷3=5. So 30 ÷ 6 = 5 groups.

practice_r2.question: A bakery has 30 donuts packed in groups of 6 (half-dozens). How many groups?
practice_r2.feedback_correct: Correct! 6 × 5 = 30, so there are 5 half-dozen groups.

practice_r2.question: Find the difference: 803 − 459.
practice_r2.explanation: 803−459: 3−9 borrow → 13−9=4; tens 9−5=4 (after lending across zero); hundreds 7−4=3. Result: 344.

practice_r3.explanation: 60 ÷ 6 = 10 students per class. 10 × 4 = 40 pencils per class. 40 × 6 = 240. Or simply: 60 × 4 = 240.
hints: Find students per class: 60 ÷ 6 = ?. Then multiply by 4 pencils each.
Or use a shortcut: total students × pencils each = 60 × 4 = ?
practice_r3.question: A school has 60 students split into 6 equal classes. Each student needs 4 colored pencils. How many colored pencils are needed in total?

hints: Step 1: if ? ÷ 6 = 8, multiply back: 6 × 8 = ? to find the mystery number.
Step 2: once you know the mystery number, divide it by 2.

feedback.correct: Correct! 6×8=48 is the mystery number, and 48÷2=24.

practice_r3.question: A mystery number ÷ 6 = 8. What is the mystery number ÷ 2?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Correct! 6×8=48 is the mystery number, and 48÷2=24.

hints: Step 2: she eats 1 plate, so plates remaining = 7 − 1. Multiply remaining plates × 6.

feedback.correct: Yes! 42÷6=7 plates. 7−1=6 plates remain. 6×6=36 cookies left.
feedback.incorrect: 42÷6=7 plates, then 7−1=6 plates remain. 6 plates × 6 cookies = 36.

practice_r3.question: Jan baked 42 cookies and put 6 on each plate. She eats one full plate. How many cookies are left?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Yes! 42÷6=7 plates. 7−1=6 plates remain. 6×6=36 cookies left.

practice_r3.explanation: 48 ÷ 6 = 8 oranges per basket. 6 − 2 = 4 baskets remain. 4 × 8 = 32 oranges.
hints: Step 2: 2 baskets are given away, so 6−2=4 remain. Multiply remaining baskets × oranges each.

feedback.correct: Right! 48÷6=8 per basket. 4 baskets remain. 4×8=32 oranges.
feedback.incorrect: 48÷6=8 per basket. 6−2=4 baskets remain. 4×8=32 oranges remain.

practice_r3.question: 48 oranges are arranged equally in 6 baskets. Then 2 baskets are given away. How many oranges remain?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Right! 48÷6=8 per basket. 4 baskets remain. 4×8=32 oranges.
